behaviors.description: Static prompt-template lint rejects phase-specific instructions copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over template text. Behavior explicitly says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies higher levels: no real OS/network/subprocess (not System), no model-output dependence (not Agentic), and no multi-step agent pipeline (not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with templates that include known phase-instruction snippets from SKILL.md and with clean templates; assert leaked templates are rejected with precise diagnostics and clean templates pass.

behaviors.description: Fully assembled prompt from real harness (after interpolation/fixtures) contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff (template + interpolation + fixture assembly), not a single function. Integration brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess beyond local in-process assembly, (b) no AI output quality dependency, (c) no multi-agent chain, so Integration is sufficient and System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text focused on assembled prompt content.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-builder pipeline with production interpolation and fixtures, then assert the final assembled prompt excludes phase-instruction leakage patterns from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: Test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context, not hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires validating model behavior is caused by loaded skill context; this is steering-effect verification, which deterministic stubs can hide. Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes a single invocation-level validation, not multiple real agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the test through the real invocation path that reads plugin/skill files, keep user-style prompt fixed, and assert live model behavior reflects skill-context guidance rather than hard-coded rules in the prompt/template.

behaviors.description: Harness runs the same user-style prompt against degraded/missing SKILL.md variants without changing prompt text.
behaviors.justification: This is harness wiring/state-injection behavior across modules (variant loading + prompt assembly), not model-judgment quality. Rejected Agentic using concrete text "can run ... without changing test prompt text": this checks invocation invariance, not output correctness; no System boundary beyond local file/module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness with baseline and degraded/missing SKILL.md variants while holding prompt text byte-identical; assert invocation succeeds and only skill-context source changes.

behaviors.description: When relevant SKILL.md section is removed/degraded, behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on live model behavior changing when skill guidance regresses; mocks would mask this and pass. Rejected Workflow because each check is per invocation outcome under same prompt, not a chained multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral test with unchanged user-style prompt on baseline skill and degraded skill; assert baseline passes structural expectations and degraded variant fails.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before JSON parsing/extraction.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering/handoff across harness components (capture -> parse), typically with local filesystem artifact writing. Rejected System: behavior text only requires pre-parse capture ordering, which is reproducible with in-process modules and temp files; no real network/subprocess requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Execute harness path with a model response (including malformed JSON), force parser/extractor failure, and assert raw output artifact was written first and contains untouched raw text.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural constraints (schema paths, role-specific placement), not just keyword checks.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is assertion logic itself (pure structural validation rules). Rejected higher levels because behavior text targets assertion semantics, not infrastructure or model output: no OS/network/subprocess, no live AI dependence, no multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test assertion helpers with crafted payloads where keywords are present but schema path/role placement is wrong; assert structural validators fail while keyword-only checks would pass.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions missed by keyword-only checks.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says "under live model execution" and evaluates whether real model outputs trigger structural-failure cases that keyword checks miss; mocks hide this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because this is a single-response validation pattern, not multiple agentic steps chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model tests on prompts likely to produce superficially keyword-matching but structurally invalid outputs; verify structural assertions fail those cases while a keyword-only baseline would incorrectly pass.
